Paper Mario: Sticker Star was one of my favourite childhood games. I don't know why, but something about this game fascinated me so I played it again and again. But at some point, and I think it was when I heard about the other Paper Mario games and saw Let's Plays about them, I lost interest in the game over time. When I finally got Color Splash, I completely lost interest in the game. As the years went by, I became more and more critical of myself because I was no longer a small child, which completely changed my entire perspective on the Paper Mario series and I now understood what was happening with Paper Mario and the community surrounding the series. I played Sticker Star for the last time in 2021 and unfortunately found that the game is bad both objectively and from my perspective and I think everyone here knows why. But even though I don`t like the game anymore I can understand it why some people like Sticker Star because I liked it before. I don't know why, but Sticker Star has a unique, charming, really likeable but also homely vibe that I only felt in this game, which is also the only real reason why you might like this game and I respect that absolutely. Hate Sticker Star for really, really, REALLY good reasons, love Sticker Star for the vibe, I'm completely fine with it. But yes, I personally don't like Sticker Star anymore, but I'm still kind of happy that I played the game because my child self had a lot of fun with it and that's what counts most in the end, just having fun playing games...

The only Problem being, its fundamentaly flawed. A normal RPG Fighting System works like this: you beat enemies to get stronger so you can defeat the boss and progress. This Games Fighting System works like this: you find Stuff you can either use to just defeat the Boss and progress OR you can use it to fight Enemies that usually give you an equivalent of less stuff as reward then when you went into the fight, So we now have a game that has no Story and Fights that you simply run away from. i must apologize i seem to have gotten the wrong impresion of this Game. i thought it was supposed to be an RPG but it seems like this is actually a walking simulator. If you ask me why i like Mario the least out of him, Luigi, Wario and Waluigi, my answer would be that he used his one wish to bring this piece of **** back to life. Do I really need to elaborate? It’s pretty clear how awful this game is. I mentally was not able to finish the final boss because of how soul-draining Sticker Star is. Before crapping on it fully, I do like a lot of the music and it does look pretty nice for a 3DS game. But the combat, oh what the heck happened to the combat?! You use stickers to attack but they’re all a one-time use. So when they’re gone, they are gone for good. Because of that, there’s zero incentive to fight enemies because it’s a waste of stickers and time. You just save them for bosses. This is the most playable unfun game I may have ever experienced in my life and likely ever will.

While I don’t think of it as this absolute cancer that everyone else considers it to be, I cannot deny that Paper Mario: Sticker Star is a terrible game. Paper Mario 64 is fantastic, Thousand-Year Door is a masterpiece, and Super Paper Mario is not the same type of game, but still quite solid for what it is. So…what the hell happened, Nintendo?
Admittedly, there are several redeeming qualities. For a 3DS game, Sticker Star is stunning to look at. It’s also indisputable how amazing the music is. Just listen to the Warm Fuzzy Plains! The writing is also pretty funny at times. I personally love that purple Toad in Decalburg! My big hot take on this game is that if you get used to the combat (which I’ll discuss in a second), the bosses can actually be pretty fun! I’m not even kidding!
However, even with all those positives, it still doesn’t excuse some major negatives. The first problem is the lack of interesting characters and story. The first three Paper Mario games have fantastic plot lines and super fun characters like Goombella, Koops, Vivian, Lady Bow, and Count Bleck, but Sticker Star’s plot is super generic, and Nintendo replaced all of the fun characters with Toads…for some reason. Next is the pacing. There are stupid difficulty spikes all over the place, especially if you don’t understand the last and biggest point: the battle system. Instead of the active take on traditional RPGs like Paper Mario 64 and Thousand-Year Door have, Sticker Star’s battle system has no partners, leveling up with experience, and all attacks are consumable. And no, this isn’t like Mario & Luigi: Partners in Time where all of the special (Bros) attacks are consumable items. In Sticker Star, every single attack is a one-and-done sticker. There aren’t even any basic attacks! Not only does this kill any incentive to fight enemies since they waste stickers and grant no experience, but saving the stickers for bosses isn’t always going to work since they require Thing Stickers to beat, which tend to be really tedious to get!
These mechanics completely killed what could have been a great game and burned the Paper Mario series to the ground until the Thousand-Year Door remake on the Switch. Again, while I think the few positives that there are definitely keep Sticker Star from being a 0/10, it is still a terrible game that I’m not even sure is worth playing even out of curiosity.

This game would be bad, if it didn’t have a cuisine built into the game that could feed you every second of excitement from a spoon while playing this game, I love Mario oddesey but clown me if I’m wrong, but this game gotta be the best in the world. Hot take I liked the first two games even though they **** really balls but this game took all the potential from the first two games and achieved it by getting rid of every bad thing in the first two games. The plot in this game I spent so much time connecting to the characters and their dialogue especially bowsers dialogue. This game is 1000000/10
Quite literal perfection **** from the difficulty and how was its to balance the difficulty to the open world design

Honestly I don't think this game deserves so much hate. Yes it's not as good as the previous paper mario games but this game still manages to be fun and entertaining. The graphics are cool, the characters are good enough and the whole sticker gimick is interesting and not as annoying as people say. The only issue is the lack of EXP/Levels you really don't have a good incentive to fight because you lose stickers and barely earn much coins to make them worth.

Other than that this game is really fun and definitely is worth your time.

P.S I played this game as my first paper mario game so maybe that's why I didn't hate is as much.

Actually the first Paper Mario i actually beat so i didnt have very high standards and i do have some good memories of it, enjoyed it very much as a kid and loved the art style. But seeing it back now some things just dont make sense for an RPG turn based game.
I first experienced the game when I got it for my birthday, And this game has a reason to exist in the Paper Mario series. I give it a 8/10
Nostalga carries this one. love to play this game. The Raft level **** though.

Dont really get the hate for this game, definitley the worst Paper Mario game, but that doesn't mean it's bad. yes, the sticker mechanic is a bit annoying and the game isn't as charming as it could be, but that's where my gripes end. even though I like the regular RPG gameplay of 64 and the thousand year door better, the sticker mechanic still brings a neat sense of inventory management into this game, the writing is still very charming and the level design is still pretty fun. it is the weakest in the series, but if you want a 3DS game, you can do a whole lot worse than Sticker Star
First Paper Mario which brought the series to the point where it is today. for me it doesn't matter i like RPG Paper Marios but also Action Adventure.
Goated game, way too hated just because it's not like the original, the thing items are a great addition

Awesome experience!

Nice gameplay, puzzle solving, exploration, fun, great music, exciting and actually difficult boss battles, not much to complain about!

pokemon-black-version.json.review: Best Pokemon game, and most replayable in the series by far. Game is my childhood and love how you can only catch the gen 5 pokemons.
This is what I think is the best in this game seriesThe most innovations have been made, the quality is good, and the last villain has created an unmatched fear
Pokémon Black & White is the maximum of quality in storytelling, characters,Pokedex and OST that the franchise could get. Unbelievable underestimate.

One of the best entries in the Pokemon franchise, really good lore, actually meaningful story, good curve of difficulty, antogonist that really is **** you somehow never played pokemon, this is the one I would recommend to start with.

Cannot give this version a good rating as it would be in bad faith, and would be more for Black/White 2 than for this version.

Anyone rating this higher than a 5, is being disingenuous and biased.

The most beautiful game in the Pokemon black and white franchise, it is a game with a spectacular artistic design, a game with a fascinating story, a very epic game both in its ending and with its history in general, the game is full of mysteries such as cities to discover, characters in my opinion somewhat heavy and not very iconic but I think that is compensated by their gym leaders who sincerely seem very iconic to me such as Yakon, their Pokemon designs seem to me to be among the best in the franchise although to some fans it seems like a copy of the first generation it seems to me that they have their own charisma and it is not bad that it has somewhat simple designs as long as they are pretty, in conclusion this game is an outrage both for its design and its history, I give it a rating of 84
My favourite installment in the Pokémon series. Also the one I replayed the most.

This game is one of the best Pokemon games for the DS. The new location is amazing. The only thing this game is doing wrong in my opinion is the all new Pokemon. I feel like the studio could have done much better if they used the original Pokemon that consumers were used to.
Best animations and visuals for the pokemon series.  Also the best music.  Really the only issue with this game is the pokemon designs.  I think they nailed everything else.

The game is just brilliant. The region is beautiful, the pokedex is the best in the series, the characters are well written, and Team Plasma asks us some very logical questions about the use of Pokemons. 
The only negative point is that the main characters aren't particularly memorable like Red was.
Really cool visuals, music, story, and strategy but the pacing is awkward amongst questionable game design descisions

This is everything a Pokemon game should be. Pokemon Black exceeds every expectation for a Pokemon game on the DS with visuals exceeding almost everything seen on the console. The 3d integration creates a super immersive region. The story is one of, if not, the best narrative created by Pokemon. The animations of the 2d sprites of the pokemon makes battling the best it has been on the DS. The art direction and sound design is incredible with the soundtrack providing some incredible tracks.
The best Pokemon game. Nothing more to say. The story is perfect and it is also really fun to play!

Is perfect, the idea to a “new kanto” basically a restart of the Pokemon games wasn’t good in the year ir was release but today the most Pokemon emjoyners know Pokemon white and black and white 2 and black 2 are the bests Pokemon games
Has the best soundtrack the series hands down. Box legendries are strong. A classic Pokémon experience at its best.

These game often has mixed opinions and I can see why, but I'm on the positive side. One of the biggest complaints is the new Pokemon. There is a fair majority of stinkers, but that is to be expected, especially when the game adds the most amount of new Pokemon in series history. It switches things up by making these new creatures the only ones obtainable, but it does provide a different feel from old games. The game further changes things by showing that Game Freak can, and sometimes want, to give a story to their games. The writing and characters are stellar. The villain is intimidating, and the ending is a memorable one. As with most Pokemon games is the amazing music, as there are a lot of killer songs. The game also looks pretty good for the hardware it's on. Overall, this game does a lot of things right. It has a pretty good selection of Pokemon, with some being among the best in the series. It tells a unique story that works really well with the music, characters, and atmosphere. The game, even outside of Pokemon standards in truly an "N"credible game.
This was pokemon at its peak, it started going downhill after this with X/Y, Sun/Moon and don't even get me started on the Switch games

The best story Pokémon's ever told, with some actually interesting ideas and characters for once. Rest of the game is good too, though being forced to use the pretty mid Gen 5 pokemon until the post-game was kind of lame.
I like how the game looks, the region, the characters, but it does little else for me... Still enjoyable.
Pokémon with good writing

Game Freak took a bold move with this generation, and it paid off extremely well. Animated sprites, incredible environments, and a story that is much more potent than other entries in the title, combined with a decent difficulty curve. There is much to love about Pokémon Black, and it earns it's well deserved place as the 3rd best Pokémon game.
Not sure if it's nostalgia but the pixel art and sprite design in this game is so **** beautiful omg. Story is interesting and the best of any Pokémon game.

Pokemon Black improves so many small things that in the grand scale of things, Pokemon has never been better, but there also seems to be something lost in transition from old to new. Black most certainly is a different Pokemon then from what I’m used to, and although I don’t like some changes to the series, overall it’s a good transformation, and I hope to see more of it.

Very good game, the story is among the best in the series. I really like the new Pokemon, and I underappreciated the Fifth generation in the past because of my strong nostalgia for third and fourth generation, but I like it quite a lot nowadays.

However: I must warn you to definitively get White version if you have no other available Pokemon games. It has 100 extra version exclusives not in Black because of a postgame area called White Forest (Black version has Black City instead).
Much better than Scarlet and Violet.

I  first played it in 2023 so 12 years after release date. Does it hold well today? Should it be played?
I've played it on physical console and I've had lots of fun. I've tried it previously on emulators but such approach doesn't ignite a spark of joy.
In terms of story-telling, lore and world-building this game should get straight 10 - but after reaching some point of the game near the end the grind becomes unbearably dull and legend hunting isn't as good as in previous (and later) installments in series.
Even though I've spent about 150h on this game it doesn't have factor that would make me repeat whole adventure any time in the future.
Good but im not too fond of the "only gen 4 pokemons in this game" thing, its a bit lack luster

After getting lambasted for making pokemon players try new things (ie not use charizard), I’m very glad that the playerbase has turned around on these games, because I truly believe that gen 5 is the best the series has to offer. They did an excellent job with the new pokemon- you’d always find a few new things in every route, making you feel like there’s always more to discover, and the pokemon themselves are some of the most well-designed in the series, even if some of them don’t evolve until very late. The story is excellent (a first for the series), and as formulaic as it may be, I believe these games truly mastered the formula.
The pinnacle of 2D Pokemon Games, and most likely the best of all mainline pokemon games. Rich story, lore, good map design, banger music 11/10
Now this is a pokemon game right here. One of the very best ever made. So many new polemon. Great story. The sprite art is top tier. A game I'll always go back to for a new play through.

I was writing a review but got hit with a 403 forbidden and my review got removed. I'm just gonna leave it at this. tl;dr - Pokemon Black and White, as GAMES, are kinda mid. the climax was epic. the story was flawed and really boring much of the way through. the gameplay is super dull and barren for a pokemon game, almost as much so as the gen 1 games. this was disappointing for a game 5 generations in. Even games like XY people find are barebones have so much more to them in the gameplay aspect than Black and White. BW added detrimental features like the critical health theme, holding hostage the quality of each fight, the postgame was overrated, as it was simply 3 cities you explored with little incentive and not much reward. The Battle Subway is heavily lackluster in comparison to gens 3-4 battle facilities and gens 6-7 battle facilities, despite being the only battle facility in this game.

This an BORING GAME! B2W2 is so much better. The progeinsoions is so slow. The fire starter ****, the anime ****, this ****. Pokemon black is not a good game, probaly one of the worst. 4/10, and B2W2 is a 10/10

A fantastic game that was given a hard time due to people's refusal to accept change. With a brand-new cast of 156 Pokémon to catch, the largest amount introduced before or after, many people grew hostile to many of the monsters due to not being allowed to use any old favorites in the main story, which is a shame as Pokémon Black and White have the strongest story out of the entire series with the best engine to date both in and outside of battle. A region brimming with life and diversity of location and people alike, Pokémon Black and White are fantastic games that are deserving of being the series' penultimate sendoff for the Nintendo DS.
Best Pokemon game, and most replayable in the series by far. Game is my childhood and love how you can only catch the gen 5 pokemons.

pokemon-scarlet.json.review: Good game, I think the main story of the three paths could have been denser, especially the main one about the Pokémon League. The post-game story and the DLCs are good, I think they could have gone even deeper into the topic of paradoxes, like including them more in the main story, not just in the **** addition, they could have “locked” some areas so that it wouldn't be so easy to break the game. Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. I'm not asking to be able to see the characters' sweat, but I would like to see better-constructed landscapes, with little or no popping. and something they absolutely must focus on in future games is that areas with wild Pokémon should feel more alive, not just with Pokémon walking or flying around, but doing different things, just like in the anime, with Pokémon interacting with the environment, and each Pokémon with its own nature changing depending on your behavior, as well as other trainers. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.

This game is an embarrassment for a franchise that once defined handheld RPGs. On the Nintendo Switch — a console that has delivered technical marvels like Breath of the Wild and Super Mario Odyssey — this game looks and runs like a rushed beta.Performance: Constant frame drops, glitches, and visual bugs plague the experience. The open world is riddled with pop-in, broken textures, and laughably poor animations. It feels unfinished, as if Game Freak had no idea how to develop for modern hardware.Graphics: Bland environments, flat lighting, and character models that look outdated even by 3DS standards. The Switch has proven it can handle vast, beautiful worlds, but Scarlet settles for mediocrity.Gameplay: While the open-world concept had potential, it’s wasted on repetitive objectives, shallow exploration, and a lack of meaningful progression. The “freedom” is an illusion — the game funnels you into predictable battles and fetch quests.Story & Characters: Forgettable, uninspired, and padded with dialogue that adds nothing. Rivals and NPCs are caricatures, offering no emotional weight or memorable arcs.Overall Polish: Scarlet feels like a cash grab, banking on the Pokémon brand rather than delivering a product worthy of the Switch era.Verdict: Pokémon Scarlet is the worst kind of disappointment — a flagship title that fails to meet even basic standards of quality. On a console capable of breathtaking experiences, Scarlet stands out as a shameful reminder of how far the series has fallen.

It is decent if you play it on Switch 2, if you don't, it's a disgrace. The story is very fun, the graphics are bad but at least colorful, having an open world pokemon game is great but the world is also kind of empty except for the creatures.
Thanks to the Switch 2, it's now perfectly playable, now you can enjoy how bad it is at 60 FPS. And even then, it doesn't run perfectly in Area 0. What a terrible game.

I always thought I loved the Pokémon franchise, but Pokémon Scarlet showed me that wasn't quite the case. The game starts out interesting, the first steps give the impression that it's going to be something special. But when you compare the "open world" with Pokémon Legends: Arceus, it becomes clear that Scarlet is worse, even being the most **** addition, the game has many bugs and technical problems. Even after patches to fix them, almost nothing was resolved. The biggest problem, however, is the player's freedom. You can explore, but you are not significantly rewarded, which makes the experience **** the end, it seems that the franchise learned nothing from The Legend of Zelda, which showed how to give freedom and reward each choice. Scarlet starts well, but quickly gets lost in technical flaws and design **** one point, I just wanted this suffering to end.
ABSOLUTE PEAK; WHAT IS THAT ZELDA GAME. THIS IS THE MASTERPIECE EVERYONE SHOULD TALK ABOUT
Just when you thought Gamefreak couldn't make a cheaper game than Sword and Shield, they do.

A very hollow and grotesque experience. An amorphous collection of bile masquerading as a piece of entertainment software. It is as if a misshapen lump of digital flesh gained the ability to hate. Despite its clear disdain for the mortal coil along with everything good and holy in this world, it still struggles along, twisting and pulsating to maintain circulation and erratically convulsing past any brief respite of respiratory arrest. It has no mouth yet it must scream.

The technical side is completely outdated—it honestly feels like playing a PS2 game. The level design is awful, with a huge but empty and confusing map. Dungeons are basically nonexistent, just simple corridors. Pokémon are ridiculously small, so you spend more time watching your feet than exploring the world, which you’ll never really remember anyway. The Sierra Napada area is terrible, a real insult to players. Cities are totally empty, and NPCs have nothing interesting to say. The camera control is horrible. Most battles end up with the camera clipping through the ground, walls, or simply missing half of the Pokémon on screen. The lack of voice acting is becoming more and more painful, especially during key story moments. The idea of having three main questlines is nice, but it’s ruined by the lack of automatic level scaling. You can easily end up fighting a gym leader with level 20 Pokémon when your team is already level 50. Gym challenges are extremely dumb—the Psychic-type gym is probably the worst example. Characters are cute and well designed, but there are no side quests or events to interact with them, so you never get attached to anyone like in older games. The teachers are a bit more present since you attend classes and can talk to them between lessons—my favorite is Guaro. Pokémon models look bad; you can literally see the polygons. The new Terastalization mechanic is awful, especially compared to old systems like Gigamax or Mega Evolutions. The game is way too easy and lacks any difficulty settings. The idea of giving you the legendary Pokémon at the start is nice, but it’s not enough to save the experience. The post-game is almost nonexistent—basically locked behind paid DLC. In short: a game with some good ideas, but poorly executed in every way.

Okay, I have to be honest. I'm sending my copy of Pokemon Scarlet and Violet back. As a long-time fan, it's a real ****'s talk about the technical stuff first because you can't ignore it. This game is rough. I'm not just talking about the graphics looking a bit dated, I'm talking about the game slowing down constantly in the open world, especially around the lake. I had Pokemon and trees pop into existence right in front of me all the time. And the bugs... I once got stuck inside a rock and had to fast travel out. Another time, the game just froze during a picnic. It feels less like I'm playing a finished game and more like I'm testing a broken early **** the world itself feels so empty and lazy. Take the big main city, Mesagoza. It's huge, but it's all for show. You can't go into almost any of the buildings. Every street looks the same. Compare that to a place like Goldenrod City from the Gold and Silver days, which felt alive with a department store, a radio tower, the Magnet Train station. Mesagoza is just a pretty backdrop, and a repetitive one at that. The open world is just a bunch of empty fields. Where's the clever level design like the twisty paths of Mt. Coronet from Diamond and Pearl?But what really gets me is how the stories are told. The game has some serious moments, especially with Arven and his sick dog, Mabosstiff. But having these emotional scenes play out with just text boxes and characters staring blankly at each other is just awkward. It completely kills the mood. Games from the Nintendo DS era like Pokemon Black and White had way more compelling stories and characters without needing voice acting, but Scarlet and Violet's dialogue feels flat and forgettable in **** just feels like the developers cut every corner they could. For the most successful media franchise on the planet, this is not good enough. We've seen them make beautiful, polished games with heart on much weaker consoles. Playing this just makes me feel like they don't think we deserve better.

I have a lot of problems with this game, and I believe most of it starts with throwing away the things that sword and shield did right. The art style went from anime style that fit Pokémon to a dull color palette and uncanny semirealism that makes the protagonists look like porcelain dolls. The customization nose dived going from fun outfits with cute skirts, hats, heart pupils and vibrant hair colors that let me express the fairy type trainer I am, to now suddenly losing my individuality with a schooluniform and dozens of helmets. The world also suffers. Most of would is just empty grass lands with nothing noteworthy. No magical mushroom forests, volcanic caverns, desert dunes, ruins, reefs, large rivers. There is also severe Lack of environmental features like bushes, boulders; flowers, tall grass, trees and everything else. Most of the buildings you can’t interact with and half the ones you can you can’t enter and pull up menus instead. Lastly I find team stars story to drag the game down. The message is preachy with unsatisfying conclusion. It doesn’t help that this was the third playground bully team in row. 

A Pokémon game is about going on an adventure and most the aspects that immerse the player were neglected and team star as antagonist did nothing to push our imagination.

Unlimited exp candies is a ridiculously stupid thing to add, it just makes the game way too easy if you dont want to work. Don't even get me started on how bad the textures look and how poorly the game runs. New pokemon designs are uninspired. It used to take TPC a month to design each pokemon, now they just throw slop at a wall and don't even wait to see what sticks. What a disappointment.

Good game, I think the main story of the three paths could have been denser, especially the main one about the Pokémon League. The post-game story and the DLCs are good, I think they could have gone even deeper into the topic of paradoxes, like including them more in the main story, not just in the **** addition, they could have “locked” some areas so that it wouldn't be so easy to break the game. Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. I'm not asking to be able to see the characters' sweat, but I would like to see better-constructed landscapes, with little or no popping. and something they absolutely must focus on in future games is that areas with wild Pokémon should feel more alive, not just with Pokémon walking or flying around, but doing different things, just like in the anime, with Pokémon interacting with the environment, and each Pokémon with its own nature changing depending on your behavior, as well as other trainers. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.

This game is an embarrassment for a franchise that once defined handheld RPGs. On the Nintendo Switch — a console that has delivered technical marvels like Breath of the Wild and Super Mario Odyssey — this game looks and runs like a rushed beta.Performance: Constant frame drops, glitches, and visual bugs plague the experience. The open world is riddled with pop-in, broken textures, and laughably poor animations. It feels unfinished, as if Game Freak had no idea how to develop for modern hardware.Graphics: Bland environments, flat lighting, and character models that look outdated even by 3DS standards. The Switch has proven it can handle vast, beautiful worlds, but Scarlet settles for mediocrity.Gameplay: While the open-world concept had potential, it’s wasted on repetitive objectives, shallow exploration, and a lack of meaningful progression. The “freedom” is an illusion — the game funnels you into predictable battles and fetch quests.Story & Characters: Forgettable, uninspired, and padded with dialogue that adds nothing. Rivals and NPCs are caricatures, offering no emotional weight or memorable arcs.Overall Polish: Scarlet feels like a cash grab, banking on the Pokémon brand rather than delivering a product worthy of the Switch era.Verdict: Pokémon Scarlet is the worst kind of disappointment — a flagship title that fails to meet even basic standards of quality. On a console capable of breathtaking experiences, Scarlet stands out as a shameful reminder of how far the series has fallen.

It is decent if you play it on Switch 2, if you don't, it's a disgrace. The story is very fun, the graphics are bad but at least colorful, having an open world pokemon game is great but the world is also kind of empty except for the creatures.
Thanks to the Switch 2, it's now perfectly playable, now you can enjoy how bad it is at 60 FPS. And even then, it doesn't run perfectly in Area 0. What a terrible game.

I always thought I loved the Pokémon franchise, but Pokémon Scarlet showed me that wasn't quite the case. The game starts out interesting, the first steps give the impression that it's going to be something special. But when you compare the "open world" with Pokémon Legends: Arceus, it becomes clear that Scarlet is worse, even being the most **** addition, the game has many bugs and technical problems. Even after patches to fix them, almost nothing was resolved. The biggest problem, however, is the player's freedom. You can explore, but you are not significantly rewarded, which makes the experience **** the end, it seems that the franchise learned nothing from The Legend of Zelda, which showed how to give freedom and reward each choice. Scarlet starts well, but quickly gets lost in technical flaws and design **** one point, I just wanted this suffering to end.

A very hollow and grotesque experience. An amorphous collection of bile masquerading as a piece of entertainment software. It is as if a misshapen lump of digital flesh gained the ability to hate. Despite its clear disdain for the mortal coil along with everything good and holy in this world, it still struggles along, twisting and pulsating to maintain circulation and erratically convulsing past any brief respite of respiratory arrest. It has no mouth yet it must scream.

The technical side is completely outdated—it honestly feels like playing a PS2 game. The level design is awful, with a huge but empty and confusing map. Dungeons are basically nonexistent, just simple corridors. Pokémon are ridiculously small, so you spend more time watching your feet than exploring the world, which you’ll never really remember anyway. The Sierra Napada area is terrible, a real insult to players. Cities are totally empty, and NPCs have nothing interesting to say. The camera control is horrible. Most battles end up with the camera clipping through the ground, walls, or simply missing half of the Pokémon on screen. The lack of voice acting is becoming more and more painful, especially during key story moments. The idea of having three main questlines is nice, but it’s ruined by the lack of automatic level scaling. You can easily end up fighting a gym leader with level 20 Pokémon when your team is already level 50. Gym challenges are extremely dumb—the Psychic-type gym is probably the worst example. Characters are cute and well designed, but there are no side quests or events to interact with them, so you never get attached to anyone like in older games. The teachers are a bit more present since you attend classes and can talk to them between lessons—my favorite is Guaro. Pokémon models look bad; you can literally see the polygons. The new Terastalization mechanic is awful, especially compared to old systems like Gigamax or Mega Evolutions. The game is way too easy and lacks any difficulty settings. The idea of giving you the legendary Pokémon at the start is nice, but it’s not enough to save the experience. The post-game is almost nonexistent—basically locked behind paid DLC. In short: a game with some good ideas, but poorly executed in every way.

Okay, I have to be honest. I'm sending my copy of Pokemon Scarlet and Violet back. As a long-time fan, it's a real ****'s talk about the technical stuff first because you can't ignore it. This game is rough. I'm not just talking about the graphics looking a bit dated, I'm talking about the game slowing down constantly in the open world, especially around the lake. I had Pokemon and trees pop into existence right in front of me all the time. And the bugs... I once got stuck inside a rock and had to fast travel out. Another time, the game just froze during a picnic. It feels less like I'm playing a finished game and more like I'm testing a broken early **** the world itself feels so empty and lazy. Take the big main city, Mesagoza. It's huge, but it's all for show. You can't go into almost any of the buildings. Every street looks the same. Compare that to a place like Goldenrod City from the Gold and Silver days, which felt alive with a department store, a radio tower, the Magnet Train station. Mesagoza is just a pretty backdrop, and a repetitive one at that. The open world is just a bunch of empty fields. Where's the clever level design like the twisty paths of Mt. Coronet from Diamond and Pearl?But what really gets me is how the stories are told. The game has some serious moments, especially with Arven and his sick dog, Mabosstiff. But having these emotional scenes play out with just text boxes and characters staring blankly at each other is just awkward. It completely kills the mood. Games from the Nintendo DS era like Pokemon Black and White had way more compelling stories and characters without needing voice acting, but Scarlet and Violet's dialogue feels flat and forgettable in **** just feels like the developers cut every corner they could. For the most successful media franchise on the planet, this is not good enough. We've seen them make beautiful, polished games with heart on much weaker consoles. Playing this just makes me feel like they don't think we deserve better.

I have a lot of problems with this game, and I believe most of it starts with throwing away the things that sword and shield did right. The art style went from anime style that fit Pokémon to a dull color palette and uncanny semirealism that makes the protagonists look like porcelain dolls. The customization nose dived going from fun outfits with cute skirts, hats, heart pupils and vibrant hair colors that let me express the fairy type trainer I am, to now suddenly losing my individuality with a schooluniform and dozens of helmets. The world also suffers. Most of would is just empty grass lands with nothing noteworthy. No magical mushroom forests, volcanic caverns, desert dunes, ruins, reefs, large rivers. There is also severe Lack of environmental features like bushes, boulders; flowers, tall grass, trees and everything else. Most of the buildings you can’t interact with and half the ones you can you can’t enter and pull up menus instead. Lastly I find team stars story to drag the game down. The message is preachy with unsatisfying conclusion. It doesn’t help that this was the third playground bully team in row. 

A Pokémon game is about going on an adventure and most the aspects that immerse the player were neglected and team star as antagonist did nothing to push our imagination.

Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.

super-mario-bros-wonder.json.review: The only thing that's somewhat lame, making this game not a 9 or a 10, is the 'wonder' system. The first time you play the game and see the wonder effect, it's like, "WOW! This is so cool and wacky!" Then you replay the game again, and it's like, "Eh, I've seen this already; who cares?" Every time you replay the game, it loses its spectacle factor (also, having one save file associated with a Nintendo account instead of multiple save files is pretty dumb, and it SLIGHTLY pisses me off). But aside from all of that, this game is probably one of the best Mario games of all time. Absolutely get this game now!
If it was just a bit longer... I feel like it could be in the top 3 best 2d mario platformers of all time

this is a new take on the try and true NEW super Mario bros formula in a fun way it feels like Nintendo said lets take super Mario 3d world and make a new 2d game based off of that instead of the old Mario games it is a fresh breath of what is a dead genre of 2d platformers and even more specifically a fresh breath into 2d mario
A really great and fun game which makes the super mario bros games fun again, definitely try it out!

I just want to start with that when I first saw the announcement trailer for this game, I had no interest in buying it. After a while, I started to gain more interest in the game and decided to buy it. Wow.This games name is really accurate. It really gives a sense of wonder to all the levels (especially the music levels). I had a feeling of joy the whole time and was only sad when it ended.

The only thing I dislike is that characters do not bump on each other in multiplayer, which in previous games created some of the funniest times i've had with my friends in a videogame. It also makes multiplayer all too easy. Also it would have been nice to be able to ride Yoshi out of single player. Besides that, the developers' boundless creativity in making this game is simply overwhelming. Definitely the best Mario game since the legendary Mario World.
Amazing. You can see that the whole Nintendo creative team was involved in this game. Very, very good.

This game innovates on the New Super Mario formula. It adds new powers and the ability to "wonder" around freely in the world map to select your level. The levels on their own are quite interesting, but the wonder mechanics are what adds a new spin to the series. The wonder fruits allow the developers to let loss and explore new mechanics that will not traditionally fit on a normal Mario experience (like a POV change, or game genre change). The wonder fruits really kept my single player experience enjoyable and worth my time. I will continue to play the game on multiplayer, since is fun and chaotic with my family. I will see if in the future I muster the strength to get the master level courses, but the control set up hurts my thumbs, so I would like a way to customize them. Anyway, overall a great experience.

Super Mario Bros. Wonder is a spectacular evolution of the 2D formula that finally breaks free from the "New" series' predictability. The Wonder Effects inject a sense of joyous unpredictability into every level, constantly subverting expectations with surreal transformations and creative set pieces. With its vibrant art style, expressive animations, and the addition of the Badge system for customizable gameplay, it feels like the most inventive Mario adventure in years. It is a nearly flawless masterpiece of platforming design that belongs in every Switch library.

Well, I think my biggest mistake with this game was having high expectations for a genre that’s already pretty saturated and has titles like Celeste and Rayman Legends, which are both excellent games and cost a lot less. And I think one common mistake Nintendo has been making is releasing games without looking at what competitors are doing, believing that the Mario name alone will be strong enough to sell like crazy — which, unsurprisingly, is exactly what happened with this game. It sold 4 million copies in two weeks, which is pretty insane.

Metroid Dread is another example of a rather weak metroidvania if you compare it to indie games like Hollow Knight or Ender Lilies, which offer a much better experience at a much lower price than Metroid does ($60). Both of these genres are oversaturated in today’s gaming market, which makes it hard to innovate. And I also think Nintendo doesn’t really bother innovating much in these two styles anymore — their focus on innovation seems to be with their 3D games.

Comparing it to the last 2D Mario game, New Super Mario Bros. 2 on the 3DS, Mario Wonder feels like a breath of fresh air. But for me, it still doesn’t surpass what the first New Super Mario Bros. (the one on the DS) meant at the time. Of course, back then the platformer genre wasn’t as overcrowded as it is today. I also think the fact that Wonder’s price doesn’t really match what it delivers ($40 would’ve been more fair) plays a big role too.

And it’s worth mentioning that even though some of the music stages are one of this Mario’s supposed innovations, Rayman Legends does it way better and in a much more memorable way — especially with how it uses well-known songs given a unique twist, like ‘Eye of the Tiger.’ Also, most of the ‘innovations’ that happen with the Wonder Flower have already been done either in other indie games or even by Nintendo itself. For example, the stages with giant shadows — that idea already existed in some levels of Donkey Kong Tropical Freeze. And honestly, in terms of difficulty, Tropical Freeze is way ahead of Mario Wonder.

That pretty much sums up my biggest criticism of Mario Wonder: even though it’s a fun game, it doesn’t innovate in any meaningful way. The only fresh moments come from the Wonder Flower segments. Its short length also makes it hard to recommend at full price ($60) when there are platformers like the two I mentioned before that are longer, cheaper, and more inventive. Still, I’d rate it an 7 out of 10. I think the media reviews were extremely overblown — I honestly feel a lot of those high scores came more from people’s nostalgia than the game’s actual quality. It even got nominated for Game of the Year in 2023, and honestly, titles like Hogwarts Legacy or even Nintendo’s own Pikmin 4 deserved that spot way more.

So, to sum it up, it’s one of the best 2D Mario games, but not an excellent platformer overall — mainly because 2D Mario feels far behind the competition. I’d actually recommend Tropical Freeze if you want a 2D Nintendo platformer, since it goes on sale more often and offers a more fitting challenge for people used to this genre.

But I think Nintendo’s biggest marketing success was leaning heavily on nostalgia and saying in interviews that this would be a sequel to Super Mario World on the SNES (which many consider the best Super Nintendo game ever), while finally trying to create a new formula for Mario since New Super Mario Bros. on the Nintendo DS back in 2006

This is an incredible Mario game.  All of the things you love about classic Mario games are found in this game.  The innovations, and changes such as wonder seeds are zany and imaginative.  The level designs are all so unique, it felt like you were experiencing something brand new every single level, let alone every world.  The characters were charming, and the new Mario character mechanics were fun and useful.  After playing every single main line (side scroller) Mario game, I can confidently say that this is the best one so far without a doubt.  Highly recommend if you like Mario games!

I'm so done with 2D Super Mario games. They were novel before, but even with so many new features, this game just feels so boring and mundane compared to the 3D ones. It's just too many random events and sequences thrown together. Oh yay, another random event. Oh yay, another random sequence. Not really fun anymore.
This has a complete other style in comparison to the other mario bros games but it does NOT dissapoint
This is a very interesting and dinamic game to play for all the public, it's not like the classics game of this saga. This game is different than the others and this is good.

I really enjoyed this game honestly, there was a lot of charm within this 2D Mario and Ilove to see 2D Mario make a comeback, my only complainy is that theres really only 2 Bosses Bowser & Bowser Jr, other then that Solid new entry!

I knew I am bored with 2D Mario but Wonder promised so much. I was mildly entertained for 20 levels, but then I got annoyed by the forced good vibes and the ultimately boring wonder effects. 

It has creativity written all over it, but it‘s just consumption. It feels like dopamine milking until you are dry.

I liked some of the design elements: the sideways, not sideways 3D model of Mario for example.

Definitely a HUGE breath of fresh air from the carbon copies of each other that were the later NSMB games. Vibrant graphics, better job at modernising the 2D Mario formula for it's generation, not to mention the Wonder Seeds being a very neat mechanic. I'd love to see where Nintendo goes with the 2D Mario series from this point, and this game is proof that Nintendo can still make them the way they did back in the 80s and 90s.

Okay, hear me out here. I don’t think the game is that good. I suppose there is the main mode, but there’s really nothing more to do here. I feel like they overhyped the game, when I eventually got my hands on it, I was just overall dissapointed. It’s not in any means a bad game,I just feel like everyone was saying it’s so great, that it just felt gray. I was expecting a masterpiece, but got something okay at best. It’s not the best game the worlds seen, just face it.

The animation is absolutely phenomenal, the level design is so creative, its like they put a million different ideas they had since the forgettable wii u game into a blender and it came out as an amazing smoothie. 

The way it slowly ramps up in difficulty, world after world is seamless with no difficulty spikes. 

This game also has marvellous spectacle, piranhas will sing, there is rock concerts, you get to play as a goomba, what more could you want?

I suppose my only criticisms are that the map could have been better presented visually and easier to navigate and also i feel like the soundtrack isn't as strong as some other entries in the series.

A truly wonderful game.

Such a breath of fresh air after we’ve been getting just NSMB for the past decade. So much love and care was put into this game with the fantastic visuals, great level design, the great new power ups, literally everything. The only thing is that the bosses are pretty disappointing since you just fight Bowser Jr a bunch of times. The final boss is really good though even if it is easy. I also wish that the game just had multiple save fields instead of having to make new users or deleting your existing save if you want to replay the game. But other than that, this is a fantastic game and I would recommend it.

I’ve just 100% completed Super Mario Bros. Wonder and it’s easily one of the most refreshing 2D Mario experiences in years. The creativity, level design, and sheer charm kept me hooked from start to finish. The difficulty ramps up beautifully, and while most levels felt fair, I nearly lost my mind (and almost broke my Pro Controller) on Badge Marathon: The Final-Final Test. That challenge alone made the completion so much more rewarding. Score: 10/10

Super Mario Bros. Wonder is a fantastic platforming adventure that, while not the best in the series, is a vital and refreshing step forward.

The game's standout feature is the the "Wonder Flowers." These moments are a masterclass in game design, completely subverting expectations and transforming levels in bizarre, hilarious, and visually spectacular ways. This constant sense of surprise injects a much-needed shot of innovation into the 2D Mario formula. 

The new power-ups, like the incredible Elephant Mario, are also great additions that feel perfectly at home in the Mario **** core platforming is a solid, reliable foundation. It's the classic Mario experience you know and love, with tight controls and satisfying level design that makes every jump feel just right. 

This familiarity is a strength, not a weakness, and it makes the game instantly accessible to both veterans and newcomers. The inclusion of characters like Yoshi and Nabbit, who take no damage, is a thoughtful addition that makes the game highly accessible for children and new players, ensuring that everyone can experience the magic.

However, the game's overall score reflects the fact that it doesn't quite reach the heights of the series' greatest hits. While the Wonder Flowers are brilliant, the traditional platforming sections between them can feel a bit safe and by-the-book. The game’s relatively low difficulty and repetitive boss battles also prevent it from being a truly challenging experience for seasoned **** the end, Super Mario Bros. 

Wonder is a joyous and essential game. It's a testament to the fact that there's still so much room for new ideas within the 2D Mario framework. While it may not dethrone the best games in the franchise, it's an important and incredibly fun entry that successfully revitalizes the series for a new generation.

Every time you replay the game, it loses its spectacle factor (also, having one save file associated with a Nintendo account instead of multiple save files is pretty dumb, and it SLIGHTLY pisses me off). But aside from all of that, this game is probably one of the best Mario games of all time. Absolutely get this game now!

A truly wonderful game.

Such a breath of fresh air after we’ve been getting just NSMB for the past decade. So much love and care was put into this game with the fantastic visuals, great level design, the great new power ups, literally everything. The only thing is that the bosses are pretty disappointing since you just fight Bowser Jr a bunch of times. The final boss is really good though even if it is easy. I also wish that the game just had multiple save fields instead of having to make new users or deleting your existing save if you want to replay the game. But other than that, this is a fantastic game and I would recommend it.
This is one of the best 2D platformers ever. The feature of the Wonder Flower is really neat and the levels are awesome. I also like that you can play as Luigi, Peach, and even more characters.

I’ve just 100% completed Super Mario Bros. Wonder and it’s easily one of the most refreshing 2D Mario experiences in years. The creativity, level design, and sheer charm kept me hooked from start to finish. The difficulty ramps up beautifully, and while most levels felt fair, I nearly lost my mind (and almost broke my Pro Controller) on Badge Marathon: The Final-Final Test. That challenge alone made the completion so much more rewarding. Score: 10/10

Super Mario Bros. Wonder is a fantastic platforming adventure that, while not the best in the series, is a vital and refreshing step forward.

The game's standout feature is the the "Wonder Flowers." These moments are a masterclass in game design, completely subverting expectations and transforming levels in bizarre, hilarious, and visually spectacular ways. This constant sense of surprise injects a much-needed shot of innovation into the 2D Mario formula. 

The new power-ups, like the incredible Elephant Mario, are also great additions that feel perfectly at home in the Mario **** core platforming is a solid, reliable foundation. It's the classic Mario experience you know and love, with tight controls and satisfying level design that makes every jump feel just right. 

This familiarity is a strength, not a weakness, and it makes the game instantly accessible to both veterans and newcomers. The inclusion of characters like Yoshi and Nabbit, who take no damage, is a thoughtful addition that makes the game highly accessible for children and new players, ensuring that everyone can experience the magic.

However, the game's overall score reflects the fact that it doesn't quite reach the heights of the series' greatest hits. While the Wonder Flowers are brilliant, the traditional platforming sections between them can feel a bit safe and by-the-book. The game’s relatively low difficulty and repetitive boss battles also prevent it from being a truly challenging experience for seasoned **** the end, Super Mario Bros. 

Wonder is a joyous and essential game. It's a testament to the fact that there's still so much room for new ideas within the 2D Mario framework. While it may not dethrone the best games in the franchise, it's an important and incredibly fun entry that successfully revitalizes the series for a new generation.

The only thing that's somewhat lame, making this game not a 9 or a 10, is the 'wonder' system. The first time you play the game and see the wonder effect, it's like, "WOW! This is so cool and wacky!" Then you replay the game again, and it's like, "Eh, I've seen this already; who cares?" Every time you replay the game, it loses its spectacle factor (also, having one save file associated with a Nintendo account instead of multiple save files is pretty dumb, and it SLIGHTLY pisses me off). But aside from all of that, this game is probably one of the best Mario games of all time. Absolutely get this game now!
If it was just a bit longer... I feel like it could be in the top 3 best 2d mario platformers of all time

this is a new take on the try and true NEW super Mario bros formula in a fun way it feels like Nintendo said lets take super Mario 3d world and make a new 2d game based off of that instead of the old Mario games it is a fresh breath of what is a dead genre of 2d platformers and even more specifically a fresh breath into 2d mario
A really great and fun game which makes the super mario bros games fun again, definitely try it out!

I just want to start with that when I first saw the announcement trailer for this game, I had no interest in buying it. After a while, I started to gain more interest in the game and decided to buy it. Wow.This games name is really accurate. It really gives a sense of wonder to all the levels (especially the music levels). I had a feeling of joy the whole time and was only sad when it ended.

The only thing I dislike is that characters do not bump on each other in multiplayer, which in previous games created some of the funniest times i've had with my friends in a videogame. It also makes multiplayer all too easy. Also it would have been nice to be able to ride Yoshi out of single player. Besides that, the developers' boundless creativity in making this game is simply overwhelming. Definitely the best Mario game since the legendary Mario World.
Amazing. You can see that the whole Nintendo creative team was involved in this game. Very, very good.

This game innovates on the New Super Mario formula. It adds new powers and the ability to "wonder" around freely in the world map to select your level. The levels on their own are quite interesting, but the wonder mechanics are what adds a new spin to the series. The wonder fruits allow the developers to let loss and explore new mechanics that will not traditionally fit on a normal Mario experience (like a POV change, or game genre change). The wonder fruits really kept my single player experience enjoyable and worth my time. I will continue to play the game on multiplayer, since is fun and chaotic with my family. I will see if in the future I muster the strength to get the master level courses, but the control set up hurts my thumbs, so I would like a way to customize them. Anyway, overall a great experience.

Super Mario Bros. Wonder is a spectacular evolution of the 2D formula that finally breaks free from the "New" series' predictability. The Wonder Effects inject a sense of joyous unpredictability into every level, constantly subverting expectations with surreal transformations and creative set pieces. With its vibrant art style, expressive animations, and the addition of the Badge system for customizable gameplay, it feels like the most inventive Mario adventure in years. It is a nearly flawless masterpiece of platforming design that belongs in every Switch library.

Well, I think my biggest mistake with this game was having high expectations for a genre that’s already pretty saturated and has titles like Celeste and Rayman Legends, which are both excellent games and cost a lot less. And I think one common mistake Nintendo has been making is releasing games without looking at what competitors are doing, believing that the Mario name alone will be strong enough to sell like crazy — which, unsurprisingly, is exactly what happened with this game. It sold 4 million copies in two weeks, which is pretty insane.

Metroid Dread is another example of a rather weak metroidvania if you compare it to indie games like Hollow Knight or Ender Lilies, which offer a much better experience at a much lower price than Metroid does ($60). Both of these genres are oversaturated in today’s gaming market, which makes it hard to innovate. And I also think Nintendo doesn’t really bother innovating much in these two styles anymore — their focus on innovation seems to be with their 3D games.

Comparing it to the last 2D Mario game, New Super Mario Bros. 2 on the 3DS, Mario Wonder feels like a breath of fresh air. But for me, it still doesn’t surpass what the first New Super Mario Bros. (the one on the DS) meant at the time. Of course, back then the platformer genre wasn’t as overcrowded as it is today. I also think the fact that Wonder’s price doesn’t really match what it delivers ($40 would’ve been more fair) plays a big role too.

And it’s worth mentioning that even though some of the music stages are one of this Mario’s supposed innovations, Rayman Legends does it way better and in a much more memorable way — especially with how it uses well-known songs given a unique twist, like ‘Eye of the Tiger.’ Also, most of the ‘innovations’ that happen with the Wonder Flower have already been done either in other indie games or even by Nintendo itself. For example, the stages with giant shadows — that idea already existed in some levels of Donkey Kong Tropical Freeze. And honestly, in terms of difficulty, Tropical Freeze is way ahead of Mario Wonder.

That pretty much sums up my biggest criticism of Mario Wonder: even though it’s a fun game, it doesn’t innovate in any meaningful way. The only fresh moments come from the Wonder Flower segments. Its short length also makes it hard to recommend at full price ($60) when there are platformers like the two I mentioned before that are longer, cheaper, and more inventive. Still, I’d rate it an 7 out of 10. I think the media reviews were extremely overblown — I honestly feel a lot of those high scores came more from people’s nostalgia than the game’s actual quality. It even got nominated for Game of the Year in 2023, and honestly, titles like Hogwarts Legacy or even Nintendo’s own Pikmin 4 deserved that spot way more.

So, to sum it up, it’s one of the best 2D Mario games, but not an excellent platformer overall — mainly because 2D Mario feels far behind the competition. I’d actually recommend Tropical Freeze if you want a 2D Nintendo platformer, since it goes on sale more often and offers a more fitting challenge for people used to this genre.

But I think Nintendo’s biggest marketing success was leaning heavily on nostalgia and saying in interviews that this would be a sequel to Super Mario World on the SNES (which many consider the best Super Nintendo game ever), while finally trying to create a new formula for Mario since New Super Mario Bros. on the Nintendo DS back in 2006

I'm so done with 2D Super Mario games. They were novel before, but even with so many new features, this game just feels so boring and mundane compared to the 3D ones. It's just too many random events and sequences thrown together. Oh yay, another random event. Oh yay, another random sequence. Not really fun anymore.

I knew I am bored with 2D Mario but Wonder promised so much. I was mildly entertained for 20 levels, but then I got annoyed by the forced good vibes and the ultimately boring wonder effects. 

It has creativity written all over it, but it‘s just consumption. It feels like dopamine milking until you are dry.

I liked some of the design elements: the sideways, not sideways 3D model of Mario for example.

I’m probably gonna get a lot of hate for this but this game is just fine to me, idk to me I died twice my entire play through, I’m not saying I want souls borne difficulty but at least something, and levels feel a lot shorter compared to other titles. Also to me the art direction is honestly not my thing, and the new power ups don’t really do anything for me. I think this game is fine I just fail to see the major appraisal this game receives.

Okay, hear me out here. I don’t think the game is that good. I suppose there is the main mode, but there’s really nothing more to do here. I feel like they overhyped the game, when I eventually got my hands on it, I was just overall dissapointed. It’s not in any means a bad game,I just feel like everyone was saying it’s so great, that it just felt gray. I was expecting a masterpiece, but got something okay at best. It’s not the best game the worlds seen, just face it.

I liked some of the design elements: the sideways, not sideways 3D model of Mario for example.

I’m probably gonna get a lot of hate for this but this game is just fine to me, idk to me I died twice my entire play through, I’m not saying I want souls borne difficulty but at least something, and levels feel a lot shorter compared to other titles. Also to me the art direction is honestly not my thing, and the new power ups don’t really do anything for me. I think this game is fine I just fail to see the major appraisal this game receives.

Okay, hear me out here. I don’t think the game is that good. I suppose there is the main mode, but there’s really nothing more to do here. I feel like they overhyped the game, when I eventually got my hands on it, I was just overall dissapointed. It’s not in any means a bad game,I just feel like everyone was saying it’s so great, that it just felt gray. I was expecting a masterpiece, but got something okay at best. It’s not the best game the worlds seen, just face it.

This game innovates on the New Super Mario formula. It adds new powers and the ability to "wonder" around freely in the world map to select your level. The levels on their own are quite interesting, but the wonder mechanics are what adds a new spin to the series. The wonder fruits allow the developers to let loss and explore new mechanics that will not traditionally fit on a normal Mario experience (like a POV change, or game genre change). The wonder fruits really kept my single player experience enjoyable and worth my time. I will continue to play the game on multiplayer, since is fun and chaotic with my family. I will see if in the future I muster the strength to get the master level courses, but the control set up hurts my thumbs, so I would like a way to customize them. Anyway, overall a great experience.

Well, I think my biggest mistake with this game was having high expectations for a genre that’s already pretty saturated and has titles like Celeste and Rayman Legends, which are both excellent games and cost a lot less. And I think one common mistake Nintendo has been making is releasing games without looking at what competitors are doing, believing that the Mario name alone will be strong enough to sell like crazy — which, unsurprisingly, is exactly what happened with this game. It sold 4 million copies in two weeks, which is pretty insane.

Metroid Dread is another example of a rather weak metroidvania if you compare it to indie games like Hollow Knight or Ender Lilies, which offer a much better experience at a much lower price than Metroid does ($60). Both of these genres are oversaturated in today’s gaming market, which makes it hard to innovate. And I also think Nintendo doesn’t really bother innovating much in these two styles anymore — their focus on innovation seems to be with their 3D games.

Comparing it to the last 2D Mario game, New Super Mario Bros. 2 on the 3DS, Mario Wonder feels like a breath of fresh air. But for me, it still doesn’t surpass what the first New Super Mario Bros. (the one on the DS) meant at the time. Of course, back then the platformer genre wasn’t as overcrowded as it is today. I also think the fact that Wonder’s price doesn’t really match what it delivers ($40 would’ve been more fair) plays a big role too.

And it’s worth mentioning that even though some of the music stages are one of this Mario’s supposed innovations, Rayman Legends does it way better and in a much more memorable way — especially with how it uses well-known songs given a unique twist, like ‘Eye of the Tiger.’ Also, most of the ‘innovations’ that happen with the Wonder Flower have already been done either in other indie games or even by Nintendo itself. For example, the stages with giant shadows — that idea already existed in some levels of Donkey Kong Tropical Freeze. And honestly, in terms of difficulty, Tropical Freeze is way ahead of Mario Wonder.

That pretty much sums up my biggest criticism of Mario Wonder: even though it’s a fun game, it doesn’t innovate in any meaningful way. The only fresh moments come from the Wonder Flower segments. Its short length also makes it hard to recommend at full price ($60) when there are platformers like the two I mentioned before that are longer, cheaper, and more inventive. Still, I’d rate it an 7 out of 10. I think the media reviews were extremely overblown — I honestly feel a lot of those high scores came more from people’s nostalgia than the game’s actual quality. It even got nominated for Game of the Year in 2023, and honestly, titles like Hogwarts Legacy or even Nintendo’s own Pikmin 4 deserved that spot way more.

So, to sum it up, it’s one of the best 2D Mario games, but not an excellent platformer overall — mainly because 2D Mario feels far behind the competition. I’d actually recommend Tropical Freeze if you want a 2D Nintendo platformer, since it goes on sale more often and offers a more fitting challenge for people used to this genre.

But I think Nintendo’s biggest marketing success was leaning heavily on nostalgia and saying in interviews that this would be a sequel to Super Mario World on the SNES (which many consider the best Super Nintendo game ever), while finally trying to create a new formula for Mario since New Super Mario Bros. on the Nintendo DS back in 2006

I'm so done with 2D Super Mario games. They were novel before, but even with so many new features, this game just feels so boring and mundane compared to the 3D ones. It's just too many random events and sequences thrown together. Oh yay, another random event. Oh yay, another random sequence. Not really fun anymore.

I knew I am bored with 2D Mario but Wonder promised so much. I was mildly entertained for 20 levels, but then I got annoyed by the forced good vibes and the ultimately boring wonder effects. 

It has creativity written all over it, but it‘s just consumption. It feels like dopamine milking until you are dry.

I liked some of the design elements: the sideways, not sideways 3D model of Mario for example.

I’m probably gonna get a lot of hate for this but this game is just fine to me, idk to me I died twice my entire play through, I’m not saying I want souls borne difficulty but at least something, and levels feel a lot shorter compared to other titles. Also to me the art direction is honestly not my thing, and the new power ups don’t really do anything for me. I think this game is fine I just fail to see the major appraisal this game receives.

I do not recommend this game. The multiplayer is awful, the music is very forgettable, and the level design is utter trash. Levels feel barren and empty without the wonder seeds, and the platforming is brain dead. The wonder seeds become very predictable after the first few levels, and the ideas they introduce are never expanded upon. Ideas are abandoned as soon as they are introduced,  and it feels as if tiktok was a video game. Also, the controls are a general downgrade from the previous entry in the series, as Mario's signature momentum based controls are abandoned in favor of megaman style controls, where the character can change directions at a whim and instantly be going at max speed in that direction.
Terrible. Felt like the game was on drugs with intentionally weird gameplay. Waste of money.

This game is a huge cash grab peace of garbage I got this game and I was so excited but all I knew it was a cocomelon Mario game it was enough when I saw Mario turn into a elephant and say wowie zozie the only thing wowie zozie was how quick I stopped playing
I don't get it. It's a novel enough idea for a gameplay loop... but it just really goes nowhere?

The best mario game i play in my life, i start to play this game in the wii when i have 5 years old and its amazing

Incredibly disappointing sequel. I really enjoyed the first game and maybe the first fifth of this game. It quickly rehashes a lot of ideas the first game had and never really feels like a true sequel. It also forces you to do a lot more BS to get to the ending as just finishing all the unique level stars isn't enough, you have to do almost all the prankster stars as well.

Some of the level design is  made for a character that controls much better than Mario. A decent amount of trial and error that is just frustrating. This comes from unclear goals or just poor level design. The game also is a step down in theming. Rosalina's cool space station ship from the first game is gone, and now you have a tiny Mario head that takes about 2 minutes to explore and there isn't much here. Even the level select is lazier as it is just a tradition Mario path through levels instead of the cool galaxy view they had in the first game.

For the boss fights, like the first game, a lot of repeats. Some stage fights are decent and unique, but the big Bowser fights all repeat. Even the last fight is a repeat fight, just with one extra bonus easy phase to it. But thinking back, Bowser fights have always been repeated in these 3D Mario games and never that good really.

Overall, while there are some decent levels and the game looks great for a Wii game, there is just an abundance of issues here I cannot overlook. Even the decent levels are like B, B-'s. I was rating levels in my head and I think maybe 1 level out of the 71 stars I got was an A tier level. Lots of D's in there and that doesn't make a great game. 6.3/10

A fantastic follow-up to one of my favorite games of all time! I slightly prefer the first one, probably because it was so revolutionary, and felt so new.
But the inclusion of Yoshi as well as many new innovative stages, makes this game as much of a must play as the first one!

It's an improvement on its predecessor in every way, and I appreciate the ideas behind it, it's clearly one of the best platformers ever made. However, being forced to use the Wii's motion controls didn't convince me. Sometimes it felt imprecise and got tiring, plus I've noticed I'm less and less inclined to play platformers, I don't enjoy them as much as I used to.

Super Mario Galaxy 2 is one of the finest 3D platformers ever made—packed with unforgettable moments, clever design, and that unmistakable Mario magic. It's a must-play for fans of the genre and one of the best Mario games of all time.

A really good game I love it as well been just I'd still prefer the first one more though but I still think this game is still a must play the first one is like Mankind's greatest creation and the two is like the sequel to Mankind's Greatest creation

I still can’t understand how this game gets such high reviews. It’s by far the weakest 3D Super Mario ever made. Boring, repetitive, and soulless. Having to go through the same levels more than once is just painful. There’s no story, and it feels like a messy collection of random stages with no direction at all. Such a shame, because the first one was absolutely wonderful.

Did amazing job as a sequel of an already wonderful title. We need Super Mario Galaxy 3 and soon! Like please Nintendo

An incredible sequel to an already incredible game. Still has amazing gameplay, controls, galaxies, and I’ll admit a better post game with the green stars. I do prefer the first game because of the story since those cinematic cutscenes are just incredible to watch. I do understand why someone would prefer this one though and I would recommend playing this.

This game is one of the few sequels that surpasses its predecessor. Such an excellent game. Level desiign at its peak, the gameplay is better, the graphics are pretty darn good, the atmosphere had more color variety, the bosses are spectacular and much better, the pacing was faster... Most of the game is better. The best Nintendo game of the 2010 decade after Breath of the Wild.
This is one of the greatest games I have ever played in my life. It is unbelievably fun, the levels are creative, it is just overall such a great and memorable experience.
